item.title: Iran threatens war "beyond the region" if U.S. launches new attacks
item.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guard says any new U.S. or Israeli attacks will be met with retaliation "in places you cannot even imagine."

item.title: Assistant principal "did nothing" when told about 6-year-old with gun, prosecutor says
item.description: A prosecutor in the trial of a former assistant principal facing criminal charges over a 2023 school shooting said she dismissed concerns about a gun in a student's bag.

item.title: U.S. soldier fears newly freed wife could still be deported by ICE at any time
item.description: U.S. Army Sgt. 1st Class and Afghanistan war veteran Jose Serrano told CBS News his wife, Deisy Rivera Ortega, was detained by ICE despite doing the "right thing."

item.title: Raúl Castro indictment expected to be announced in Miami today, sources say
item.description: Raúl Castro is being indicted on charges related to Cuba's deadly 1996 shootdown of planes operated by humanitarian group Brothers to the Rescue, U.S. officials told CBS News earlier this month.

item.title: U.S. Army soldier worries his wife could be deported despite release from ICE custody
item.description: A U.S. Army soldier and Afghanistan war veteran says he's worried that his newly-freed wife could still be targeted by ICE and deported. Camilo Montoya-Galvez reports.

item.title: WHO says Ebola risks high for region, but not a "pandemic emergency"
item.description: WHO chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus says risks from the Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda are "high at the national and regional levels, and low at the global level."

item.title: Suspected drug trafficker rescued from plane crash linked to politician
item.description: A survivor of a recent plane crash near Florida was allegedly found with roughly $30,000 inside a bag labeled with the name of a Bahamian politician.

item.title: The major cases the Supreme Court will decide in the coming weeks
item.description: The Supreme Court's term is set to end around the end of June, with decisions on birthright citizenship, transgender athlete bans and gun rights still to come.

item.title: Cheaper, alternative health plans are having a moment, but critics urge caution
item.description: Congress' decision not to extend enhanced marketplace tax credits has boosted the appeal of alternative health coverage with lower monthly premiums.

item.title: Trump says he was "an hour away" from striking Iran
item.description: President Trump says he scrapped a planned attack on Iran at the request of Gulf allies as "serious negotiations" on a peace deal are underway.

item.title: Live updating Pennsylvania 2026 primary election results
item.description: Pennsylvania voters went to the polls on Tuesday, casting their votes for Republican and Democratic candidates in congressional, gubernatorial and local races.

item.title: Rep. Massie is latest GOP incumbent to lose primary after Trump backs challenger
item.description: President Trump endorsed former Navy SEAL Ed Gallrein in Kentucky's 4th Congressional District.

item.title: What to know about today's primaries in Georgia, Kentucky and more
item.description: Polls have closed for primaries in several states on Tuesday, including the most expensive House primary in history.

item.title: Mike Collins and Derek Dooley advance to runoff in Georgia GOP Senate primary
item.description: Rep. Mike Collins and former college football coach Derek Dooley will advance to a runoff next month in the Georgia GOP primary for Senate, as Republicans vie for an opportunity to take on Democratic Sen. Jon Ossoff.

item.title: Keisha Lance Bottoms wins Georgia Democratic governor primary, CBS News projects
item.description: Former Atlanta mayor Keisha Lance Bottoms has secured enough support to avoid a runoff in Georgia's closely watched Democratic primary for governor, CBS News projects

item.title: Senators grill Blanche over "anti-weaponization" fund
item.description: Senators grilled acting Attorney General Todd Blanche on Tuesday over the Justice Department's nearly $1.8 billion fund to compensate people who claim they were politically targeted. CBS News' Natalie Brand and Caitlin Huey-Burns report.

item.title: Current wave of mid-decade redistricting "never seen before in American history," expert says
item.description: The redistricting battle for the 2026 midterms will have a profound impact on the balance of power in Washington, D.C. CBS News' David Becker, Anthony Salvanto, Nikole Killion and Caitlin Huey-Burns have more.

item.title: What's behind Cuba's energy crisis?
item.description: In January, the U.S. began a blockade of oil tankers to Cuba, exacerbating an ongoing energy crisis. Roxanna Vigil, international affairs fellow in national security at the Council on Foreign Relations, joins with analysis.

item.title: Who could benefit from Trump's $1.7+ billion "anti-weaponization" fund?
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche announced the creation of the fund as part of the settlement of President Trump's lawsuit against the IRS over the leaking of his tax returns.

item.title: New video shows fiery moments engine separated from UPS plane in deadly crash
item.description: The NTSB also revealed that the crew of the crashed jet had been reassigned to it after their original plane was taken out of service.

item.title: Andy Barr projected to win Kentucky GOP Senate primary
item.description: CBS News projects that Andy Barr will win the Republican primary for Kentucky Senate. CBS News' Aaron Navarro, Caitlin Huey-Burns and Nikole Killion have more.

item.title: Watch: Rep. Thomas Massie concedes Kentucky House GOP primary to Trump-backed Ed Gallrein
item.description: Republican Rep. Thomas Massie of Kentucky on Tuesday night conceded his House primary race to Ed Gallrein, President Trump's candidate of choice. CBS News senior White House and political correspondent Ed O'Keefe joins from Massie's watch party with more. Then, political strategists Terry Sullivan and Joel Payne join with analysis.

item.title: First White House reaction to Massie's loss in Kentucky: "F--- around and find out"
item.description: Shortly after Rep. Thomas Massie's loss in the Kentucky House GOP primary on Tuesday night, White House director of communications Steven Cheung posted on social media, "Do not ever doubt President Trump and his political power. F--- around, find out." CBS News' Caitlin Huey-Burns and Nikole Killion join with analysis.

item.title: DOJ's settlement with Trump bans IRS from taking action over his old tax returns
item.description: The Internal Revenue Service is permanently barred from pursuing claims against President Trump or his company based on prior tax returns, part of a controversial settlement deal between the Justice Department and Mr. Trump.

item.title: Mosque shooting victims hailed as heroes for preventing further violence
item.description: Police and federal authorities are working to piece together the events leading up to Monday's deadly shooting at the Islamic Center of San Diego, as well as what exactly happened during the attack. CBS News' Anna Schecter has the details.

item.title: The story of Cuba's 1996 shootdown that could lead to Raúl Castro's indictment
item.description: Thirty years ago, a Cuban fighter jet shot down two civilian planes operated by Florida-based exile group Brothers to the Rescue, an incident that inflamed U.S.-Cuba relations.

item.title: Senate advances resolution to limit Trump's Iran war powers for first time
item.description: Four Republicans voted with nearly all Democrats to advance the Iran war powers measure, with GOP Sen. Bill Cassidy joining the Republican defectors for the first time.

item.title: Mangione supporters should not have been given press passes, mayor says
item.description: Luigi Mangione supporters have loudly made their feelings known outside every court appearance, but several are now in court with official press passes.

item.title: Who's who in Cuba's leadership? Here are the names to know.
item.description: President Trump has been openly mulling a takeover of Cuba similar to the operation that toppled Venezuela's Nicolás Madoro. What it would mean for the regime's leaders remains to be seen. Here are some of the figures to watch.

item.title: Grammy-winning artist Jason Isbell talks about the craft of songwriting and his latest music
item.description: Grammy-winning singer-songwriter Jason Isbell sits down with CBS News anchor John Dickerson to discuss inspiration, gratitude and the craft of songwriting. Isbell talks about his latest music and performs an acoustic version of "Cast Iron Skillet."
